comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: DE-RISK E3 (offline) CONFIRMS the S2 refactor: 11/45 prompts interpolate PER-RUN data into the SYSTEM body — completion_verifier/code_quality/security/tests/ticket_quality use {{ticket_context}}+{{ticket_id}}; plan_review_* use {{plan}}. Cross-run caching of these requires relocating per-run data to the USER message (or a stable-vs-volatile split). NOTE: the CONTAINER system prompt's {{plan}} is the PARENT plan, which is STABLE across the per-child fan-out — which is exactly why S1 caching works (E1). So S2's refactor targets the generic gate prompts, not the container.

comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Engine-wide caching (dep S1 done); subagent code review is binding quality gate.".

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent c81c plan-review fan-out epic; this child S2 (engine-wide caching via RunnerAgentStep) reviewed whole against the full sibling roster]

4-bucket audit of the parent's acceptance/success criteria, mapping S2's contribution and cross-checking every absence against the roster (S1 0250, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34):

- "caching applied across plan-review AND all workflow agent steps" (success criterion) — FULLY covered HERE for the engine-wide leg. S1 explicitly fences out RunnerAgentStep ("Out: engine RunnerAgentStep caching (S2)"), so S2 is the SOLE sibling covering verify/decide/coach/grounding + completion-verification + code-review caching. Clean producer→consumer split with S1 (S1 owns the runner cache mechanism; S2 owns RunnerAgentStep prefix-wiring + the stable-vs-volatile prompt-model convention).
- "materially lower token cost (cache reads non-zero)" — PARTIAL here (S2 adds engine-step cache reads: AC `cache_read > 0` on repeats for verify/decide/coach + completion-verification + code-review). The plan-review container token win is S1's domain. Covered by roster.
- "8-child epic review completes in minutes (wall-clock)" — STRUCTURAL/not-applicable here (S2 is engine-step caching, not the plan-review fan-out latency lever); wall-clock → sibling ba7e (S3). Covered by roster.
- "caching provider-safe: openai/gemini succeed unchanged" — PARTIAL here, correctly: S2's AC carries "unchanged on other providers", extending S1's anthropic-gating to the engine steps. Mechanism owned by S1. Covered.
- "no fidelity regression / within offline-eval tolerance" — covered HERE for the relocation risk S2 introduces: the single-pass G3-1 remediation added a FIDELITY-VERIFY AC for the 11 relocated gate prompts (esp. completion-verifier, which backs the mandatory close gate, + code-review) — no longer asserted. Epic-wide semantic eval → sibling da34 (S6). Covered.
- "make lint + suite pass" — per-child portion covered here (AC: "make lint + tests pass").
- "all six child stories closed" — aggregate/structural; not a single-child mapping.

No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the union of children, and S2 introduces no parent-criterion it then leaves uncovered (the relocation-fidelity risk it creates is closed by its own G3-1 fidelity AC). G3: CLEAN — no findings for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c-cbcd-17a0-43d2 (plan-review fan-out optimization). Reviewed THIS child (S2, engine-wide caching via RunnerAgentStep) WHOLE against the parent and the complete sibling roster (S1 0250, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34). Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes. NO G4 FINDINGS.

Affirmations:
- Implicit shared state / consumer impact: S2 consumes S1's runner cache mechanism (anthropic_cache_instructions) at the SHARED runner seam, and extends it to RunnerAgentStep (workflow/runs.py:154-217 build_agent_request). Producer→consumer is DAG-ordered (S1->S2 dep link present). The split is clean — S1 owns the runner-seam cache-flag mechanism; S2 owns RunnerAgentStep prefix-wiring + the stable-vs-volatile prompt-model convention + the volatile-data relocation. S1's own G4 review reciprocally affirms this split.
- Conflicting assumptions: S2's mechanism is correctly DISTINCT from S1's container case, not contradictory. S1 caches the system_prompt= kwarg directly because the container {{plan}} is already byte-stable; S2's workflow system prompt is NOT byte-stable (it interpolates per-run ticket_id/ticket_context — verified at workflow/runs.py:131-146 resolve_prompt, with generic/default `instructions`), so S2's MAJOR-6 remediation correctly adds the relocation step S1 did not need. S2's de-risk E3 comment explicitly reconciles the two domains (stable container {{plan}} = S1; volatile gate-prompt {{ticket_context}} = S2). No contradiction with S1 or the parent's "cache the SYSTEM PROMPT … no RunRequest content-list change" remediation.
- Dependency gap / ordering: S2 depends only on S1 (link present); it is in the fidelity-neutral tranche (S1,S3,S2 ship ahead of eval-gated S4/S5); no eval-gate ordering obligation, no gap.
- Scope overlap: S2 fences out S1 (runner mechanism) and S3 (parallelization); S1 reciprocally fences out RunnerAgentStep caching ("Out: engine RunnerAgentStep caching (S2)"). No overlap on the shared files (runner.py = S1; workflow/runs.py + prompts.py = S2).
- Residual references: no sibling carries a contract against a design S2 removed; the only relocation S2 performs is reconciled against S1/S6 via de-risk E3 and the G3-1/G4-A fidelity-verify AC.

Cross-child consumer-impact note that is already CLOSED (not a finding): S2 relocates per-run data across 11 workflow prompts including the completion-verifier that backs THIS project's mandatory close gate (and code-review). The single-pass G3/G4 remediation (G3-1/G4-A) already converted the bare "no finding change" assertion into a FIDELITY-VERIFIED AC (reuse parity.py recall/false-accept or a targeted spot eval). Because S2 self-remediates this risk, it is not a live G4 finding for this pairing.

Non-G4 editorial note (intra-child, below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold; reads benignly under ANTI-FP): the S2 body still uses the old epic-era "cacheable-prefix boundary … after the CachePoint / a separate message part" framing, which the parent's S1 remediation and S2's own MAJOR-6 relocation supersede (the operative mechanism is: move volatile ticket data to the USER message + mark the stable/volatile split, then anthropic_cache_instructions over the now-stable system prefix). This is stale terminology within one child, not a cross-child inconsistency — recorded for editorial cleanup only.

Affirmations:
- Implicit shared state / consumer impact: S2 consumes S1's shared-runner cache mechanism (anthropic_cache_instructions, runner.py:421-435) and extends it to the engine RunnerAgentStep (workflow/runs.py:207 build_agent_request). S1 caches the container system_prompt directly because the {{plan}} prefix is already byte-stable; S2's workflow system prompt is NOT byte-stable (interpolates per-run ticket_id/ticket_context at workflow/runs.py:131-146 resolve_prompt), so S2's MAJOR-6 remediation correctly adds the relocation step S1 did not need. Consistent with the parent's "cache the SYSTEM PROMPT … no RunRequest content-list change" remediation.
- Dependency gap / ordering: S2 depends only on S1 (link present); it sits in the fidelity-neutral tranche (S1,S3,S2 ship ahead of eval-gated S4/S5). No eval-gate ordering obligation, no gap. The strict S3->S4->S5 sizing.py serialization does not touch S2 (S2 edits workflow/runs.py + prompts.py + parity.py, not pass1.py/sizing.py).

- Consumer-impact note already CLOSED (not a finding): S2 relocates per-run data across 11 workflow prompts including the completion-verifier that backs THIS project's mandatory close gate (and code-review). The single-pass G3/G4 remediation (G3-1/G4-A) already converted the bare "no finding change" assertion into a FIDELITY-VERIFIED AC (reuse parity.py recall/false-accept or a targeted spot eval). Self-remediated ⇒ not a live G4 finding.

Non-G4 editorial note (intra-child, below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold; benign under ANTI-FP): the S2 body still uses the old epic-era "cacheable-prefix boundary … after the CachePoint / a separate message part" framing, which the parent's S1 remediation and S2's own MAJOR-6 relocation supersede (operative mechanism: move volatile ticket data to the USER message + mark the stable/volatile split, then anthropic_cache_instructions over the now-stable system prefix). Stale terminology within one child, not a cross-child inconsistency — recorded for editorial cleanup only.

compiled_state.description:
# Engine-wide caching: extend the runner cache to RunnerAgentStep (all workflow agent steps)

## Why
S1 caches plan-review's Pass-1 calls. The same stable-prefix waste exists in every workflow agent step
(verify/decide/coach/grounding) and other workflows (completion-verification, code-review) — they re-send a
stable system prompt + tool surface across runs. Extending caching there is the engine-wide win pulled into scope.

## What
- In the engine `RunnerAgentStep` (`workflow/runs.py:207`, builds RunRequest), populate S1's cacheable-prefix
  boundary: the static system prompt (+ stable inputs) is the cached prefix; per-run ticket data is the
  volatile suffix.
- Add a prompt-model convention to mark which part of an assembled workflow prompt is the byte-stable prefix vs
  per-run volatile inputs, so the boundary is set generically (not per call site).
- Apply `anthropic_cache_instructions` for the system prompt across steps; keep dynamic per-run content OUT of
  the cached prefix (after the CachePoint / a separate message part).

## Scope
- In: RunnerAgentStep prefix wiring; the prompt-model stable-vs-volatile convention; applying to all workflows.
- Out: the runner mechanism itself (S1, dependency); container parallelization (S3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RunnerAgentStep populates the cacheable prefix; verify/decide/coach cache their stable system-prompt/tool
      prefix on anthropic (`cache_read > 0` on repeats), unchanged on other providers.
- [ ] completion-verification and code-review workflows benefit (cache reads non-zero on repeats).
- [ ] Per-run volatile inputs are excluded from the cached prefix (stability test).
- [ ] `make lint` + tests pass; no finding change.

## Remediation (opus review — MAJOR 6)
- The workflow system prompt interpolates PER-RUN ticket data (`workflow/runs.py:146 resolve_prompt`), so it is
  NOT byte-stable — `anthropic_cache_instructions` over it would miss every run. Caching requires moving volatile
  ticket data OUT of the system prompt into the USER message across workflow prompts (verify/decide/coach/
  grounding/completion-verification/code-review) and marking the stable vs volatile split in the prompt model.
- file_impact extended to the prompt model/front-matter.
- [ ] The rendered workflow system PREFIX is byte-stable across runs (a stability test); per-run ticket data is
      relocated to the user message.
- [ ] verify/decide/coach + completion-verification + code-review show `cache_read > 0` on repeats (anthropic).

## Remediation (single-pass G3/G4 review — G3-1/G4-A)
- 'No finding change' for the 11 relocated prompts is currently ASSERTED, not measured — and one is the
  completion-verifier that backs this project's mandatory close gate. Add a fidelity check.
- [ ] The relocated gate prompts (esp. completion-verifier + code-review) are FIDELITY-VERIFIED: they produce
      equivalent findings post-relocation (reuse parity.py recall/false-accept, or a targeted spot eval) — not asserted.

file_impact.reason: RunnerAgentStep cacheable prefix + relocate volatile data
fidelity-verify relocated gate prompts (no finding change)

compiled_state.title: Engine-wide caching: extend the runner cache to RunnerAgentStep (all workflow agent steps)
